I had been in depression for at least four months already. For a long time I felt nothing but loneliness. I was simply alone and needed by no one. I was being eaten up inside by a sense of emptiness, and the only thing I did, apart from going to school, was lie on my bed in complete darkness.
It is so important to open up and be there for people. No matter what they are battling. Sit through the really difficult moments even when you feel like you aren’t doing your best, keep showing up.

You prolly won’t read this but I hope you do growth is uncomfortable, but so is staying stuck. Choose the discomfort that moves you closer to the person you want to become. Stay consistent. Stay hungry. And when you look back, you’ll realize that the moments you almost quit were the ones that mattered most.

Depression may be hinted at through physical signs: teary eyes (very dry from crying) skin issues like acne, a flat or sad facial expression, tired eyes, slumped posture, slower movements, and eary grey dull or thinning hair. These signs alone aren't diagnostic, and professional evaluation is essential for accurate assessment.
